steps.title: Use quadratic regression for the product
steps.content: The product of two linear expressions is quadratic. Evaluate $g(x)h(x)$ at three x-values and fit $ax^2+bx+c$.

steps.title: Fit the quadratic
steps.content:
Run the regression row:
$$y_1\sim ax_1^2+bx_1+c$$
Desmos returns $a=\frac{18}{5}$, $b=4$, and $c=-\frac{35}{6}$.

steps.title: Confirm by multiplying
steps.content:
Distribute:
$$\left(\frac{3}{5}x\right)(6x)=\frac{18x^2}{5}$$
$$\left(\frac{3}{5}x\right)(-5)=-3x,\quad \left(\frac{7}{6}\right)(6x)=7x$$
The x-terms combine to $4x$, and the constant is $-\frac{35}{6}$. So:
$$g(x)h(x)=\frac{18x^2}{5}+4x-\frac{35}{6}$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: That expression matches choice D, so D is correct.
